message:
fix(copilot): prevent false-positive path matches with segment-based comparison

The old `looks_like_copilot_storage()` used substring matching on the
entire path string, causing directories like `/home/user/projects/copilot-research`
to be misidentified as Copilot Chat storage. This rewrites the detection to
compare individual path segments against known Copilot directory names
(`github.copilot-chat`, `copilot-chat`, `gh-copilot`) and adds a two-segment
window check for the nested CLI config path `~/.config/gh/copilot`.

Also sorts module declarations and imports alphabetically (copilot after
claude_code) and reformats long conditionals in probe.rs to satisfy rustfmt.

- Adds negative test for `copilot-research` false positive
- Reformats parse_conversation_file, extract_turn_timestamp, title derivation,
  and platform-specific path construction per rustfmt
- Sorts `pub mod` declarations in connectors/mod.rs alphabetically
- Reflows import list in indexer/mod.rs to match new module order

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
